commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(codex): scope Computer Use empty approval content
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

This PR scopes the Computer Use empty-form approval response so OpenClaw returns an explicit machine-readable approval payload only for configured Codex Computer Use approval elicitations whose requested form has no fields.

Before this patch, Computer Use empty-form approvals returned a contentless accept:

```json
{ "action": "accept", "content": null, "_meta": null }
```

After this patch, only the Computer Use empty-form path returns:

```json
{ "action": "accept", "content": { "approve": true }, "_meta": null }
```

Non-Computer Use empty-schema MCP approvals keep the existing contentless accept shape, reducing compatibility risk.

## Why / Motivation & User Impact

Related to #96452. This PR intentionally does not claim to close that issue.

Review and live-canary feedback showed the earlier patch improved the focused approval-bridge behavior but did **not** prove or fix the later live `computer-use/get_app_state` 120s native `tools/call` timeout. The useful OpenClaw-owned change here is narrower: after the existing OpenClaw approval flow allows a Codex Computer Use empty-form approval, OpenClaw returns explicit approval content instead of `null`.

Codex still owns native MCP helper execution after approval. Merge judgment for this PR should be based on whether maintainers want this narrow Computer Use approval-response compatibility mitigation, not on it fully resolving #96452.

## Implementation

- Adds an optional `emptySchemaAcceptContent` bridge field.
- Sets that field only for configured Computer Use approval elicitations.
- Clones the configured empty-schema accept content per response instead of returning the shared constant object directly.
- Preserves legacy/contentless accepts for other empty-schema Codex MCP approval elicitations.
- Preserves deny/cancel behavior unchanged.
- Preserves fail-closed handling for unmappable non-empty schemas.
- Adds focused boundary coverage for both paths:
  - Computer Use empty-form approvals return `{ "approve": true }`.
  - Non-Computer Use empty-form approvals remain contentless.
- Updates Computer Use docs to state the Computer Use-specific boundary and that Codex still owns later native `tools/call` execution.

## Review Context Addressed

- ClawSweeper compatibility concern: addressed by narrowing `{ "approve": true }` to configured Computer Use empty-form approvals only.
- Non-Computer Use boundary: covered by a regression test that preserves the existing contentless empty-schema accept shape.
- Shared-payload defensive hardening: latest follow-up clones the Computer Use accept payload per response, avoiding any accidental cross-response mutation of the static content object.
- PollyBot13 live canary feedback: incorporated by removing closing syntax for #96452 and documenting that the later live native `tools/call` timeout remains unproven/unfixed by this PR.
- Latest ClawSweeper proof request: still treated as the remaining merge-readiness blocker for live behavior. A Mantis visual proof request was posted in PR discussion to try to capture current-head live behavior for the approval payload and follow-on `get_app_state` result.

## Maintainer / ClawSweeper Review Addressed

- [Resolved] Scope the protocol-facing payload change: only configured Computer Use empty-form approvals now receive `{ "approve": true }`; unrelated empty-schema MCP approvals still return `content: null`.
- [Resolved] Add regression coverage for the compatibility boundary: focused tests cover both Computer Use and non-Computer Use empty-form approval responses.
- [Resolved] Avoid overstating #96452 coverage: body and docs now say this is an approval-response mitigation, not the canonical native `tools/call` timeout fix.
- [Resolved] Tighten implementation quality: latest follow-up returns a fresh shallow copy of the configured empty-schema accept content per accepted response.
- [Pending / limitation] Current-head live proof that exercises the real Discord-routed Codex Computer Use path is still pending; the existing evidence proves the bridge response shape and CI/body-proof gates, not the later native `get_app_state` timeout.

## Evidence

Current head: `76dfaaf456d15e3621c82262d9ae9548f998bf2d`.

GitHub Actions / repository checks:

- PR evidence policy check passed on the current head: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28216635535
- Full CI/check matrix for the current head was triggered by the latest push and is expected to replace earlier green checks as jobs complete.
- Previous full CI passed on the prior scoped head `8467e8f8e958e765f796d62341ba5ff8a86a711d`: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28190606724

Focused proof and static gates:

- Local pre-check only: `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` passed for the latest follow-up.
- The diff includes regression coverage preserving non-Computer Use empty-form approvals as contentless accepts.
- Earlier focused proof before the scope-tightening follow-up passed on the proof branch:
  - Run: https://github.com/snowzlmbot/openclaw/actions/runs/28111159509
  - Command:

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.test.ts \
  -t "Computer Use|empty form schema"
```

Live canary feedback incorporated:

- PollyBot13's live canary against `c8ee8579b311` still timed out after 120s on `computer-use.get_app_state`.
- That result is now treated as evidence that this PR should not be considered the canonical native `tools/call` timeout fix.

Not tested / limitation:

- Current-head live proof showing the scoped Computer Use empty-form approval response after an allow decision in the real Discord-routed Codex path is still pending. This PR should not be merged as a full #96452 fix unless maintainers accept it as a narrow approval-response mitigation or the pending live proof demonstrates more.

## Evidence Source Map

- GitHub Actions: current-head PR evidence policy check passed; current-head full CI is running after the latest follow-up push.
- Focused tests: existing focused test coverage exercises the changed response shape and the non-Computer Use compatibility boundary.
- Live canary: PollyBot13 canary shows the later native `get_app_state` timeout remained on an earlier head, so this PR is intentionally scoped to approval response shape only.
- Local pre-check only: whitespace/diff gate passed with `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`.
- Media / Mantis proof: requested in PR discussion; no successful proof artifact has been posted yet.

## Risk

- Compatibility: the protocol-facing accept payload changes from `null` to an object for configured Computer Use empty-form approvals only. The regression test preserves the old shape for other empty-schema approvals.
- Mutation safety: the configured payload is cloned per response, so response consumers cannot mutate the shared constant for later requests.
- Security / permissions: this does not broaden Computer Use permissions, bypass OpenClaw approval, or bypass Codex/platform permission checks. It only changes the approval bridge response after an allow decision already exists.
- Product behavior: the later native `tools/call` timeout may still occur; that remains owned by #96452 and the separate native completion fix path.

## Rollback

Revert this PR to restore contentless accepts for Computer Use empty-form approval responses. No data migration, configuration migration, or irreversible state change is involved.

## Docs Updated

- `docs/plugins/codex-computer-use.md` documents the Computer Use-specific approval response boundary and keeps native `tools/call` ownership with Codex.

## Related Issue / PR

- Related issue: #96452
- Related candidate timeout fix path noted by ClawSweeper: #96818
target.author: snowzlmbot

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
